Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
4th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Israel and Lebanon agreed to a conditional ceasefire following US-mediated talks in Washington. The deal is contingent on a complete halt to hostilities, including all Hezbollah attacks.

● Lebanese reports indicate continued IDF strikes in several southern Lebanon villages despite the announcement.

● Hezbollah mortar fire struck a UNIFIL post near Debin (Dibbine) in southern Lebanon overnight, killing one peacekeeper and wounding two. The IDF traced the launches to the Al Qatrani area.

● The US House of Representatives passed a war powers resolution (215-208) demanding an immediate halt to American military operations against Iran. President Trump dismissed the resolution.

● The IAEA Director General urged Iran to fully cooperate, explain the fate of its enriched uranium stockpiles following last year’s strikes, and resume full inspections.

END OF THE BRIEF

Manufacturing aircraft is the easy part. (Not so easy in our case). However Supporting 100–200 aircraft for the next 40 years is the hard part.

The real question isn’t whether India can build the Il-114-300 or SJ-100 under licence. It’s whether the MRO, spares, overhaul and supply-chain ecosystem can keep fleet availability high throughout its service life.

Airlines buy support as much as they buy aircraft. If an airline has an aircraft grounded in Chennai, Delhi, or Jakarta, can parts arrive within hours rather than weeks?
tweets.content:
Manufacturing aircraft is the easy part. (Not so easy in our case). However Supporting 100–200 aircraft for the next 40 years is the hard part.

The real question isn’t whether India can build the Il-114-300 or SJ-100 under licence. It’s whether the MRO, spares, overhaul and supply-chain ecosystem can keep fleet availability high throughout its service life.

Airlines buy support as much as they buy aircraft. If an airline has an aircraft grounded in Chennai, Delhi, or Jakarta, can parts arrive within hours rather than weeks?

BREAKING: Ministry of Defence has officially implemented the Delegation of Financial Powers to Defence Services (DFPDS-2026). This major policy overhaul grants enhanced financial authority for revenue-related procurement, decentralising a budget value exceeding ₹1.25 Lakh Crore annually.

- Operational Readiness: Grants field commanders direct financial autonomy to expedite decision-making and enhance immediate combat preparedness.

- Doubled Works Capital: Financial allocations for structural and infrastructure works projects are officially doubled to eliminate delays and ensure timely completion.

- Indigenisation & Innovation: Significantly expands financial ceilings for research and development to reduce reliance on foreign OEMs. This directly boosts domestic private players, including MSMEs and defense startups.

This marks a structural shift toward military decentralisation and self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
